description: Spec cites a fabricated CSA standard designation ('B64 SERIES:23'-pattern) for nitrogen-receiver registration; verification identifies that the designation does not exist and grounds the correct pressure-vessel reference. Complements tp_dc_disputed_nonexistent_standard (where the correction was uncertain, so DISPUTED).

finding.issue: The pre-action nitrogen receiver is specified to CSA B64 SERIES:23 — a designation that does not appear to exist as a published standard, and CSA B64 governs backflow preventers, not pressure vessels.
finding.replacementText: constructed to the ASME BPVC and CRN-registered per CSA B51

paragraph_map_slice.text: B. Nitrogen receiver shall be registered to CSA B64 SERIES:23.

captured_verifier_response.explanation: CSA B64 is the standard family for backflow preventers, and 'SERIES:23' is not a valid CSA edition suffix — the designation as cited does not exist. Pressure-vessel design registration for a nitrogen receiver in Canada is governed by CSA B51 (ASME BPVC construction with a Canadian Registration Number), which is the correct reference.
captured_verifier_response.correction: constructed to the ASME BPVC and CRN-registered per CSA B51

ground_truth.correct_correction_text: constructed to the ASME BPVC and CRN-registered per CSA B51
ground_truth.notes: Fabricated designation (§5.14 item 9 'B64 SERIES:23'-pattern). The verifier grounds the correct designation, so CORRECTED (not DISPUTED). Refutation is where verification earns its cost (§9 Q2).
